resources.string: Shows Auto Brightness debug flash messages system-wide (over other apps). This optional service only draws the message overlays — it never reads screen content. Enable it only if you want the diagnostic flashes visible while using other apps.
Grant \'Modify system settings\' to control brightness
Manual override active — tap Resume to continue
Auto Brightness paused — tap Resume to continue
Manual override active — open to resume
Save & load profiles · automatic context rules
To match Wi-Fi rules the app needs the connected network name (SSID). On Android the normal way to read it requires Location permission AND Location services switched on.
To match Wi-Fi rules the app needs the connected network name (SSID). On Android the normal way to read it requires Location services switched on.

You can skip the Location requirement if the app can read the name directly:

• Shizuku or root — the app runs "cmd wifi status" (works with root too).
• Otherwise, grant DUMP over ADB and the app reads "dumpsys wifi":
DUMP is a read-only diagnostics permission you grant manually over ADB (revoked when you uninstall). This app uses it only to read the Wi-Fi name.
If your SSID contains \"SSID\", this will cause problems. Sorry, not sorry.
Above the 1–100 range — this will be saved as 100.
Delete the profile “%1$s”? This can\'t be undone.
Replace “%1$s” with your current settings? The saved profile will be overwritten.
Sets the shake intensity/duration to trigger an Emergency UI Reset (hold the phone upside-down, screen on, then shake). 0 = pass-through (disables the shake requirement); 10 = long, vigorous shake.
Live data may be stale — the monitor may have stopped responding.
Circadian using a sun position cached %1$d day(s) ago — turn on Location briefly to refresh.
Circadian using default sun times (no location) — turn on Location briefly for accurate sunrise/sunset.
Showing cached sun events from %1$d day(s) ago. Turn on Location briefly — or enable the IP fallback below — to refresh sunrise/sunset.
No location yet, so circadian is using default sun times. Turn on Location briefly — or enable the IP fallback below — for accurate sunrise/sunset.
No write access — tap to set up
You changed the brightness yourself, so auto-control is paused. Resume to hand control back.
Snap the screen back to the automatic brightness and clear a manual change
Couldn\'t open the tile picker on this device
Live brightness, lux, profile and context with a quick service toggle and reset.
This project is a complete, native replacement for Android\'s stock auto-brightness system — a more intelligent, responsive, and customizable brightness experience.
Created by /u/v_uurtjevragen, who built the original in Tasker and authored this native Android rebuild.
Thanks to João Dias, creator of Tasker — the automation app the original Advanced Auto Brightness was built on.

Copyright (c) 2026 /u/v_uurtjevragen

Permission is hereby granted, free of charge, to any person obtaining a copy of this software and associated documentation files (the "Software"), to deal in the Software without restriction, including without limitation the rights to use, copy, modify, merge, publish, distribute, sublicense, and/or sell copies of the Software, and to permit persons to whom the Software is furnished to do so, subject to the following conditions:

The above copyright notice and this permission notice shall be included in all copies or substantial portions of the Software.

THE SOFTWARE IS PROVIDED "AS IS", WITHOUT WARRANTY OF ANY KIND, EXPRESS OR IMPLIED, INCLUDING BUT NOT LIMITED TO THE WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE AND NONINFRINGEMENT. IN NO EVENT SHALL THE AUTHORS OR COPYRIGHT HOLDERS BE LIABLE FOR ANY CLAIM, DAMAGES OR OTHER LIABILITY, WHETHER IN AN ACTION OF CONTRACT, TORT OR OTHERWISE, ARISING FROM, OUT OF OR IN CONNECTION WITH THE SOFTWARE OR THE USE OR OTHER DEALINGS IN THE SOFTWARE.

This project is a complete replacement for your phone\'s built-in auto brightness. Its goal is a smoother, more intelligent, and completely customizable experience that adapts to light the way you want it to.

• **Grant Permissions:** on first launch, Tideo guides you through granting notifications and write-settings permissions (essential for the app to function).
• **Activate the Service:** flip the master switch on the Dashboard. A persistent notification confirms Tideo is active and disables Android's native auto-brightness.
• **Set Your Boundaries:** Menu → Misc. Adjust Min and Max Brightness — the absolute darkest/brightest the screen will ever get.
[TIP] For OLED screens, set Min Brightness to a small value (5 or 10) instead of 0 to prevent "black crush".

All settings are reached from the Menu. **Curve & Brightness** shapes the core curve; **Reactivity** tunes sensor behaviour; **Misc** holds master limits and animations; **Circadian** scales brightness by time of day; **Super Dimming** handles ultra-low brightness and PWM flicker; **Profiles & Contexts** manage configurations and automatic switching.\n[TIP] Your secret weapon: tap the ⓘ icon next to any setting\'s label for a detailed explanation.

A three-part formula:
• **Zone 1 (darkness to dim):** Zone 1 scaling is the main low-light control; Zone 1 End is where "dark" becomes "dim".
• **Zone 2 (dim to medium):** Zone 2 scaling and offset shape the curve for common indoor light — use the graph to see the transition.
• **Zone 3 (bright):** handled automatically, smoothly approaching maximum brightness.
• **Automatic Curve Fitting:** with override detection on, Tideo saves your manual points. Gather data in ≥9 lighting conditions, then run the curve wizard and review on the graph before applying.

A smart dead zone removes fluctuations from sensor noise so the screen reacts only on significant light changes.\n• **Thresholds:** Dark, Dim and Bright control the dead-zone size (higher = more stable).\n• **Curve:** mid and slope control the smooth sensitivity transition between dim and bright.

Save the current config as a named profile and swap between strategies.\n• **Dashboard tracking:** active settings are compared to factory defaults; tuned values appear in gold.\n• **System presets:** Battery Saver and Outdoors are modifiable; the default profile is modifiable but not deletable.

Rules pair triggers with a profile; a matching trigger auto-loads it.
• **Triggers:** App, Time & Day (supports SUNRISE/SUNSET), Battery, Location (geofence), and Wi-Fi.
• **Conflicts:** higher Priority (1–100) wins; ties broken by more triggers (more specific).
• **Pausing:** manually loading a profile pauses the context engine; resume from the Profiles screen or by cycling the screen off/on.
[TIP] Location rules cost battery — prefer Wi-Fi rules over geofences where you can.

• **Dynamic Scaling (Circadian):** adjusts the whole curve by time of day relative to sunrise/sunset.
• **Super Dimming & PWM-sensitive mode:** go darker than Android's minimum via elevated access or a screen-filter overlay; PWM-sensitive mode fixes hardware brightness at a flicker-free floor and dims further with a software overlay.
[WARN] Set the PWM threshold to the **lowest** value that eliminates flicker. Too high keeps pixels at high power while dimmed by overlay, accelerating screen aging / burn-in.
• **Manual Override:** adjusting the slider pauses Tideo and posts a notification; tap Resume.
• **Panic Button:** shake the device vertically while upside down to reset, stop automation and force max brightness (S.O.S. vibration confirms).
• **Quick Settings tile:** pause/resume or toggle the service.

• **Brightness graph:** teal line = your curve, blue dots = manual override points, gold line = reference.
• **Reactivity graph:** the stability curve and the light change required to trigger an update.
• **Other graphs:** Circadian and Super Dimming visualize behaviour over time and brightness.
• **Power Draw calibration (Tools):** an experimental tool that steps through brightness levels and measures current to build a power curve.

• **Apply:** makes changes active immediately, but not written to disk — lost on a profile load or app restart.
• **Save (Profiles screen):** writes current settings to the selected profile — the only way to make changes permanent.
• **Reset:** reverts the current tab to defaults.
[TIP] After editing multiple tabs, save to your active profile; if using the context engine, overwrite the relevant profile or your changes are replaced when a rule fires.
Enjoy your new intelligent auto brightness!

Measures how much power your screen draws at each brightness level: the display fills with white and steps through brightness while Tideo samples battery current. Best with Airplane Mode on and the charger unplugged.

For accurate results:

1. Enable Airplane Mode (important)
2. Close background apps
3. Do not touch the screen during the test
4. Unplug the charger

The screen will turn white and sweep through brightness levels for ~1–2 minutes.
Battery current sensor not available on this device
Please unplug the charger and try again
No power-draw data yet — run the calibration to measure it.
Fix the highlighted curve error before applying.
Show debug/context flashes over other apps via an optional Accessibility overlay. It only draws the messages — it never reads screen content.
You have unsaved changes on this screen. Discard them and leave?
Couldn\'t acquire a location — grant Location, or enable the IP fallback below, then try again
Could not read the current Wi-Fi name
Grant Location permission to use the current location
No location fix yet — try again in a moment
Grant usage access so per-app rules can detect the foreground app
Allow notifications so the ongoing brightness controls and status are visible.
Grants brightness control. Without this the service runs but cannot change brightness.
Needed only for location-based context rules and as a fallback for reading the Wi-Fi name. Wi-Fi rules also work without it via Shizuku / ADB.
One of your context rules targets specific apps. Grant usage access so the service can detect the foreground app.
Only needed if you later add a context rule that switches profiles per app. You can skip this for now and grant it from the Profiles & Contexts screen later.

Installed outside the Play Store, Android may block the \"Modify system settings\" toggle below (and \"Usage access\", for per-app rules) — it looks greyed out or shows \"Restricted setting\". Tap it anyway; that usually shows the unlock prompt. If not, open App info → ⋮ menu → \"Allow restricted settings\", then return here.
Enables super dimming below the dimming threshold. Choose any one channel — it is a one-time grant of WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS.
Shizuku is installed but not running — start the Shizuku app, then return here.
Fits a brightness curve to your recorded manual overrides (needs ≥ 9 points).
Inertia (τ): %1$s — lower follows your points, higher stays near the current curve
Not enough recorded override points (need ≥ %1$d real points, have %2$d).
Could not fit a curve to the recorded points.
Only English is available right now. Human translations are welcome — see CONTRIBUTING.md.
Minimum wait cannot exceed maximum wait.
Dimming threshold is below minimum brightness.
Transition factor > 0.5 may produce a non-sensical curve.
Taper midpoint cannot exceed maximum brightness.
Switch to a different profile automatically based on the foreground app, Wi-Fi, time of day or charging state.
Per-app rules need usage access to detect the foreground app. Without it they never trigger.
Turn on only the conditions this rule should match. The rule loads its profile when ALL enabled triggers are satisfied.
Usage access is required to detect the foreground app.
The reactivity level in complete darkness. A higher value means the brightness is more stable and less \'jumpy\' in very dark rooms.
The baseline reactivity for most situations. A lower value makes the system more responsive to small decreases in light.
The baseline reactivity for bright, outdoor light. A higher value prevents tiny shadows (like your hand) from causing annoying brightness dips.
Controls how quickly the reactivity changes as you move from dim to bright light. Higher value = more abrupt transition.
The log-transformed lux level where the system is most sensitive to change. Think of it as the \'center point\' of the reactivity curve.
Enable this ONLY if auto-brightness seems unresponsive on your device. This may cause brightness to become unstable or flicker.
Disable this if you get frequent false positives for the override detection.
Controls how much to smooth out sensor readings. Higher values react faster to small light changes, but may increase jitter. Lower values are more stable, but might feel sluggish.
Sets the lowest brightness level the screen will ever use. Recommended to be > 0 on OLED screens to avoid \'black crush\'.
Sets the highest brightness level the screen will ever use. Can be used to cap the max brightness to save battery.
A simple brightness boost or cut. This value is added to (or subtracted from) the final brightness across all light levels.
A global multiplier for the entire brightness curve. Use it to adjust the overall contrast. >1 stretches the curve brighter, <1 compresses it dimmer.
The number of steps in a brightness change animation. More steps create a smoother, but slightly slower, transition.
The longest time (in milliseconds) between animation steps. Controls how slowly small, subtle brightness changes occur.
The shortest time (in milliseconds) between animation steps. Controls how fast large brightness changes feel.
Use notifications to keep the service alive and show \'paused\' notifications.
Controls how quickly brightness rises in dim light. Recommended: 1 to 5
Sets the lux level where \'dim light\' ends and \'indoor light\' begins. Typically for moving from a dark room to a lit one.
Higher values give a major boost in medium light. Recommended: 3 to 10
Subtle but powerful \"offset\" for the midrange curve. Lower values make the transition from dark to dim light more gradual and smooth. Recommended: -300 to zone 1 end.
Sets the lux level where \'indoor light\' ends and \'bright outdoor light\' begins. Affects the transition when going outside.
Controls the maximum super dimming strength. Value shows what happens without circadian effects.
Controls how wide the scale shifts over the day. Only active when circadian scaling is enabled!
Controls how gradual the super dimming kicks in and how it behaves as brightness approaches min brightness. <1: hard transition, not recommended. 1: linear transition. >1 soft(er) transition.
This is the screen brightness below which super dimming kicks in.
Enables or disables the entire experimental circadian scaling feature. When disabled the strength setpoint is used as a maximum value throughout the day.

Gamma-like exponent. Controls the shape of the dimming curve. Higher values keep the screen brighter for longer and fade more steeply near the threshold; lower values dim more quickly. Adjusts how brightness transitions, not how much(!)
Enables or disables the software dimming feature. When enabled the max hardware brightness is fixated to the PWM threshold.
Shift the whole curve across the day using sun position.
How wide the scale shifts over the day (%, 1–100).

Off by default. When GPS/network gives no fix and no fixed location is set, turn this on to look up an approximate location from your public IP via ipwho.is (HTTPS). This also powers \"Use current location\" below when no on-device fix is available.
Couldn\'t read this profile. It isn\'t a Tideo export or a Tasker AAB config.
Grant the Download/AAB/configs folder once to load profiles saved by the Tasker app.
Context automation is paused after a manual profile load.
These settings will replace your current configuration.
